project.name: Rafeeq
project.description: Cross-platform Quran quiz and viewer app (React + Ionic + Capacitor). Runs on Web, iOS, Android, Electron.

nodes.name: VerseVisibilityContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context providing cross-viewer verse selection and visibility (hide/show) state, with hidden verses persisted to localStorage and selection kept volatile in-memory only.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses useMemo to memoize the entire context value object, avoiding unnecessary consumer re-renders. Hidden state uses immutable Set copies to preserve referential equality checks.

nodes.name: VerseVisibilityProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider component managing verse selection and hidden sets, persisting the hidden set to localStorage on every change via useEffect.

nodes.name: useVerseVisibility
nodes.summary: Custom hook that exposes the VerseVisibilityCtx from context, throwing a descriptive error when called outside the provider.

nodes.name: useFeedbackBeep.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that synthesizes short two-tone audio beeps for quiz answer feedback (correct/wrong) using the Web Audio API, with no external audio assets.
nodes.languageNotes: Handles both standard AudioContext and the legacy webkit-prefixed variant for broad browser compatibility. AudioContext is lazily initialized on first use.

nodes.name: useFeedbackBeep
nodes.summary: Returns a memoized beep callback that plays a correct (ascending G5â†’C6) or wrong (descending A4â†’E4) tone sequence via Web Audio oscillators.

nodes.name: useWakeLock.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that acquires a Screen Wake Lock to prevent the device screen from dimming during quiz sessions, re-acquiring it when the page becomes visible again.
nodes.languageNotes: Follows the Screen Wake Lock spec requirement to re-acquire after visibilitychange events; silently ignores acquisition failures (e.g. battery saver mode).

nodes.name: useWakeLock
nodes.summary: Acquires and manages the Screen Wake Lock lifecycle, including releasing on unmount and re-acquiring when the page becomes visible.

nodes.name: arabic.util.ts
nodes.summary: Canonical Arabic text utility module providing number conversion to Eastern Arabic-Indic digits, diacritics stripping, and loose answer normalization used across all quiz and viewer surfaces.
nodes.languageNotes: Consolidates functions previously duplicated across multiple quiz files. Uses precise Unicode ranges for tashkeel and pause marks rather than broad character class matching.

nodes.name: toHindiNumbers
nodes.summary: Converts Western/ASCII digit characters to Eastern Arabic-Indic (Hindi) digit characters for RTL number display.

nodes.name: removeDiacritics
nodes.summary: Strips Arabic tashkeel diacritics and pause marks from text, used for fuzzy answer matching and search normalization.

nodes.name: normaliseAnswer
nodes.summary: Normalizes a string for loose quiz answer comparison by stripping diacritics, collapsing whitespace, and lowercasing.

nodes.name: QuizExitModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared confirmation modal component displayed when the user attempts to exit an active quiz, with localized cancel and confirm buttons.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyah.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive test page for the Akmel Al-Ayah verse-completion quiz, supporting immersive Mushaf context mode, hint progression, answer submission with sound feedback, and a results summary screen.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements an immersive mode where the question card collapses to expose the full MushafContextViewer when the user opens context; wake lock keeps the screen on during active quizzing.

nodes.name: splitVerse
nodes.summary: Splits a verse text string at approximately the midpoint into displayed and hidden portions for quiz question generation.

nodes.name: buildQuestion
nodes.summary: Builds a QuizQuestion object from a raw verse, splitting it into displayed and hidden portions and populating all required fields.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyah
nodes.summary: Main quiz test component orchestrating question loading, answer evaluation, hint progression, immersive context viewer toggling, and final score display.

nodes.name: AkmelAlNehayat.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive test page for the Akmel Al-Nehayat verse-ending completion quiz, splitting verses at the last waqf (stop) symbol rather than midpoint, otherwise sharing the same immersive quiz UX as AkmelAlAyah.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a splitAtLastWaqf function that detects Unicode waqf/stop markers in the Quran text to determine the question split point, producing a more linguistically meaningful quiz boundary than a simple midpoint split.

nodes.name: splitAtLastWaqf
nodes.summary: Finds the last waqf (Quranic stop) symbol in a verse text and splits the string there, returning displayed and hidden portions with a found indicator.

nodes.name: MutashabihatTest.tsx
nodes.summary: Interactive test page for the Mutashabihat quiz that presents groups of similar Quranic verses and challenges the user to distinguish them by completing the differing endings.
nodes.languageNotes: Unique among the quiz tests: supports a selectedVerseIdx to let the user switch the MushafContextViewer between the target verse and its similar sibling verses by tapping info-strip chips.

nodes.name: MutashabihatTest
nodes.summary: Main mutashabihat quiz component orchestrating group-based question generation, sibling verse navigation, answer checking via fuzzy matching, and immersive Mushaf context viewer.

nodes.name: mutashabihat.service.ts
nodes.summary: Service module for the Mutashabihat quiz providing algorithms to detect groups of Quran verses with shared opening phrases, filter them by scope, build question objects, and fuzzy-check user answers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a 3-word sliding window to build a phrase map; groups with non-unique completions are discarded so every question has meaningfully different answers. Fuzzy answer checking requires 60% word overlap.

nodes.name: getAllMutashabihatGroups
nodes.summary: Scans an array of Verse objects to identify groups of verses sharing the same opening 3-word phrase but differing endings, returning structured MutashabihatGroup objects.

nodes.name: filterGroupsBySurahs
nodes.summary: Filters mutashabihat groups to only include groups whose verses belong to specified surahs, keeping groups with at least 2 matching verses.

nodes.name: filterGroupsByPages
nodes.summary: Filters mutashabihat groups to only include verses within a given mushaf page range, keeping groups with at least 2 matching verses.

nodes.name: filterGroupsByJuzs
nodes.summary: Filters mutashabihat groups to only include verses belonging to specified juz numbers, keeping groups with at least 2 matching verses.

nodes.name: buildMutashabihatQuestion
nodes.summary: Builds a MutashabihatQuestion object from a group by randomly selecting a target verse, computing the displayed/hidden split, and collecting sibling verses as distractors.

nodes.name: checkMutashabihatAnswer
nodes.summary: Fuzzy-checks a user's answer against the correct hidden portion using diacritic-stripped word overlap, returning true when at least 60% of words match.

nodes.name: InlineSelect.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable inline dropdown select component that renders its option list in a DOM portal to avoid overflow clipping, with smart flip logic to open upward when near the bottom of the viewport.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses createPortal to render the dropdown list at the document body level, preventing z-index and overflow:hidden conflicts common in scroll containers.

nodes.name: InlineSelect
nodes.summary: Renders a trigger button and a portal-mounted ARIA listbox with smart up/down positioning, outside-tap dismissal, and scroll-aware close behavior.

nodes.name: MushafContextViewer.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared viewer component that renders a Mushaf page in context around a target verse, integrating hint-based word-reveal, page navigation, verse visibility state, and a big-text fallback mode detected via ResizeObserver.
nodes.languageNotes: Merges the global VerseVisibilityContext hidden set with a local revelation window so quiz hints only reveal up to the hinted word count while still respecting the user's manual hide state from the page viewer.

nodes.name: MushafContextViewer
nodes.summary: Main context viewer component managing page loading, partial verse revelation for hints, page navigation, next-verse progressive reveal, and width-based big-text mode.

nodes.name: verse.model.ts
nodes.summary: Canonical TypeScript type definitions for Quran verse data, quiz question shapes (standard and mutashabihat), and quiz configuration objects used across all quiz and viewer features.

nodes.name: ThemeContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context that manages the app-wide day/night theme, persisting the selection to localStorage and syncing it to the document's data-theme attribute.

nodes.name: ThemeProvider
nodes.summary: React component that provides day/night theme state and toggle callbacks to its subtree via context.

nodes.name: useAudioPlayer.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that manages a shared HTML Audio element for verse audio playback, exposing play/stop callbacks and reactive isPlaying/playingKey state.

nodes.name: useAudioPlayer
nodes.summary: Hook that lazily creates an Audio element, wires play/ended/pause event listeners, and returns stable play and stop callbacks along with playback state.

nodes.name: useImmersiveMode.ts
nodes.summary: Custom React hook implementing immersive reading mode: hides/shows UI chrome on tap/touch with debounce and drag-threshold detection, also tracking last interaction time.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses multiple refs as stable storage for gesture coordinates and timestamps to avoid unnecessary re-renders during touch event processing.

nodes.name: useImmersiveMode
nodes.summary: Hook that tracks pointer/touch start events and toggles chrome visibility based on tap vs drag discrimination; exposes showChrome, hideChrome, registerTouchStart, and maybeToggleOnTap callbacks.

nodes.name: AuthCallback.tsx
nodes.summary: OAuth2 redirect handler component that exchanges the authorization code for tokens, then closes the popup window so the originating tab receives tokens via a storage event.

nodes.name: AuthCallback
nodes.summary: React component that processes the OAuth callback URL, calls exchangeCodeForToken, then closes the tab or redirects to an error route.

nodes.name: AccountModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable bottom-sheet modal wrapper component for the account page, providing title, close button, backdrop click handling, and Escape key dismissal.

nodes.name: PageViewer.tsx
nodes.summary: Central Quran page viewer component orchestrating page display, touch/swipe navigation, audio playback, immersive mode, recite/quiz mode with word hints, bookmarking, and activity recording.
nodes.languageNotes: Integrates many hooks and contexts (useAudioPlayer, useImmersiveMode, useWakeLock, usePlayback, useVerseVisibility) and manages complex quiz/recite state with word-level reveal and hint logic.

nodes.name: PageViewer
nodes.summary: Main viewer component containing all page navigation, audio, quiz/recite mode, immersive mode, verse action sheet, and playback sheet state; renders the MushafPage with full interaction handling.

nodes.name: MushafPage.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared component that renders a single Madani Mushaf page using QPC V4 Tajweed COLRv1 fonts, with strict 15-line layout or big-text mode, tap/long-press verse selection, and hidden-word overlay measurement.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ResizeObserver + useLayoutEffect for pixel-perfect font sizing; hidden segment overlays are measured after DOM layout to avoid shifting the Madani line grid.

nodes.name: MushafPage
nodes.summary: React component that renders a single Quran Mushaf page with font loading, line layout, surah headers, bismillah rows, and interactive verse selection.

nodes.name: NoteModal.tsx
nodes.summary: Bottom-sheet modal for per-verse user notes, supporting list and compose views with full CRUD (add, edit, delete) backed by the user API, with login-gating and error handling.

nodes.name: NoteModal
nodes.summary: React component rendering a two-view (list/compose) notes sheet for a given verse key, managing note fetch, save, and delete operations with optimistic state updates.

nodes.name: VerseActionSheet.tsx
nodes.summary: Bottom-sheet action panel for a long-pressed verse, offering tafsir, translation, audio playback, bookmarking, and notes access with tab-based navigation and tafsir resource selection.

nodes.name: VerseActionSheet
nodes.summary: Component managing tafsir/translation fetching, audio playback via useAudioPlayer, bookmark toggling, notes modal integration, prev/next verse navigation in tafsir, and tafsir resource selection.

nodes.name: font.loader.ts
nodes.summary: Loads and caches QPC V4 Tajweed per-page woff2 fonts and the legacy bismillah TTF, injecting @font-face and @font-palette-values rules into the document and persisting blobs to IndexedDB.
nodes.languageNotes: COLRv1 color fonts are handled with @font-palette-values; a mono palette override is synthesized for when tajweed coloring is disabled.

nodes.name: ensurePageFont
nodes.summary: Ensures the QPC V4 font for a given Mushaf page is loaded into the document, with deduplication via a Set of injected pages and an inflight Map.

nodes.name: loadOrFetchBismillahFont
nodes.summary: Loads the Bismillah font blob from IndexedDB cache or fetches it from the network and stores it for future use.

nodes.name: loadOrFetchFont
nodes.summary: Loads a per-page Quranic font blob from IndexedDB or fetches it from the network with cache-first IDB persistence.

nodes.name: injectPalettesForPage
nodes.summary: Injects light and dark CSS colour-palette rules for a page's font family to enable word-by-word verse highlighting.

nodes.name: setMonoPaletteColor
nodes.summary: Rebuilds the mono @font-palette-values rules for all already-injected pages with a new literal color, called on theme change.

nodes.name: appendMonoRule
nodes.summary: Appends a CSS @font-palette-values rule for a single page font in the monochrome colour palette.

nodes.name: preloadAllPageFonts
nodes.summary: Preloads all 604 Mushaf page fonts in the background, yielding to the browser every 10 pages and reporting progress via an optional callback.

nodes.name: quran-data-provider.ts
nodes.summary: Facade that routes Quran data requests to the @quranjs/api SDK client when credentials are available, falling back transparently to the custom quran-api.client.ts HTTP client.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a generic trySdkOrFallback dispatcher that calls methods by name on either the SDK client or the fallback module.

nodes.name: trySdkOrFallback
nodes.summary: Attempts a data operation via the Quran SDK client and falls back to the REST API client on SDK unavailability or error, logging warnings on degraded paths.

nodes.name: fetchTranslationsByPage
nodes.summary: Fetches verse translations for a given page using the SDK/fallback pattern, with support for a specific translation edition ID.

nodes.name: fetchTafsirForAyah
nodes.summary: Fetches tafsir (Quranic commentary) for a specific verse using the SDK/fallback pattern, accepting surah number, verse number, and tafsir resource ID.

nodes.name: quran-sdk-client.ts
nodes.summary: Instantiates the @quranjs/api QuranClient with environment credentials or a no-op proxy when credentials are absent, and exports an isSdkAvailable guard.

nodes.name: createClient
nodes.summary: Creates and returns the quran.com SDK client wrapped in a Proxy that returns rejected promises for missing methods, enabling safe optional-method calls.

nodes.name: isSdkAvailable
nodes.summary: Returns true if the quran.com SDK is available in the current environment by checking for the global QuranAPI object.

nodes.name: quran.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core Quran data service providing page-level verse retrieval with a two-layer cache (LRU in-memory + IndexedDB), full-corpus text search with Arabic normalisation, surah/juz/page-range verse queries, tafsir/translation/audio access, and a corpus seeding pipeline from a bundled JSON file.

nodes.name: getPage
nodes.summary: Fetches all verses for a given Quran page, checking the in-memory LRU cache, then IndexedDB, and finally the remote API before caching results at both layers.

nodes.name: getAllVerses
nodes.summary: Loads all 6236 Quranic verses across all 604 pages, triggering cache repair if any pages are missing from IndexedDB.

nodes.name: getSurahVersesList
nodes.summary: Returns all verses belonging to a specific surah by fetching pages within the surah's page range from the chapter metadata.

nodes.name: getJuzVerses
nodes.summary: Fetches all verses for one or more juz numbers via the API data provider, mapping raw API verse objects to the app's internal Verse model.

nodes.name: repairPagesCache
nodes.summary: Clears and rebuilds the IndexedDB pages cache by re-fetching all pages from the API, used as a recovery mechanism when cache corruption is detected.

nodes.name: preloadAllPages
nodes.summary: Sequentially preloads all 604 Quran pages into IndexedDB, tolerating individual page failures and reporting progress via an optional callback.

nodes.name: seedTextCorpus
nodes.summary: Seeds the full-text search corpus into the IndexedDB 'verses' store from the bundled /data/quran-text.json, versioned to avoid redundant re-seeding.

nodes.name: searchQuran
nodes.summary: Searches the Quran corpus with Arabic diacritic-normalised matching â€” supports verse-key lookup (e.g. '2:255'), full-text IDB search, and page-cache fallback, returning ranked results.

nodes.name: findVerseByKey
nodes.summary: Finds a specific verse by surah and ayah number by looking up the IDB verses store and then falling back to the page cache at the estimated page.

nodes.name: IDBService
nodes.summary: IndexedDB wrapper class providing open/get/getAll/put/bulkPut/delete/clear operations for all Rafeeq data stores.

nodes.name: idb.service.ts
nodes.summary: Thin IndexedDB wrapper exposing typed get/put/delete/clear/bulkPut operations for the rafeeq-quran database, exported as a singleton idb instance.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses version upgrade logic to wipe the pages and fonts stores on upgrade to v5 (QPC V1 → V4 migration).

nodes.name: Search.tsx
nodes.summary: Quran full-text search entry page with recent-searches list, live debounced search-as-you-type, and navigation to SearchResults; persists recent queries with result counts in localStorage.

nodes.name: Search
nodes.summary: Main search page React component managing live search state, debounced quranService calls, recent-searches display, result highlighting, and navigation to SearchResults.

nodes.name: SearchResults.tsx
nodes.summary: Search results page that renders expandable verse cards for Quran search hits, supporting copy, share, and continue-reading actions that navigate to the PageViewer at the matched verse.

nodes.name: SearchResults
nodes.summary: React component rendering paginated Quran search results with expandable verse cards, share/copy actions, and navigation to the PageViewer.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: React DOM entry point that mounts the root App component inside StrictMode.

nodes.name: LanguageContext.tsx
nodes.summary: Provides a React context for app-wide language selection (Arabic/English), persisting the choice to localStorage and exposing a useLang hook for consuming translation strings.
nodes.languageNotes: Applies dir and lang attributes to document.documentElement so CSS RTL layout and font-weight adjustments work without additional JavaScript.

nodes.name: LanguageProvider
nodes.summary: Context provider component that manages current language state, persists to localStorage, and synchronizes HTML dir/lang attributes.

nodes.name: useLang
nodes.summary: Custom hook that exposes the LanguageContext value (lang, setLang, toggle, t, isRTL) to any consumer component.

nodes.name: mushaf.config.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the supported Mushaf rendering kinds (Tajweed, Uthmani, IndoPak, Imlaei) with their API word-field mappings and display labels used in Settings.

nodes.name: Azkar.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-featured Azkar (Islamic remembrances) page allowing users to browse dhikr categories, count recitations with tap gestures, trigger haptic feedback on native platforms, and persist session state to localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Capacitor Haptics with graduated intensities (Light/Medium/Heavy) and a web fallback via navigator.vibrate for cross-platform tactile feedback.

nodes.name: readAzkarFlags
nodes.summary: Reads vibration and sound flags from the shared settings localStorage key without subscribing to Settings state, enabling decoupled cross-feature preference reading.

nodes.name: haptic
nodes.summary: Dispatches haptic feedback using Capacitor Haptics on native platforms or the Web Vibration API on web, with three named feedback patterns: tick, complete, and reset.

nodes.name: loadCatState
nodes.summary: Loads per-category azkar counter and completion state from localStorage, returning safe defaults if nothing is stored.

nodes.name: Azkar
nodes.summary: Main Azkar page component that renders dhikr categories and individual azkar cards with tap-to-count, swipe-to-reset gesture recognition, haptic feedback, and completion tracking.

nodes.name: Home.tsx
nodes.summary: Home page that displays the Basmalah and app brand name as the primary landing screen, with the shared BottomNavBar.

nodes.name: Home
nodes.summary: Home page React component that renders the full-screen Basmalah display with Rafeeq branding and the bottom navigation bar.

nodes.name: QuizList.tsx
nodes.summary: Quiz selection page that displays cards for each available quiz type (AkmelAlAyah, Mutashabihat, AkmelAlNehayat) and navigates to their respective setup pages.

nodes.name: QuizList
nodes.summary: Quiz list React component rendering a grid of quiz entry cards with i18n-aware titles and descriptions, routing to quiz setup pages on tap.

nodes.name: Settings.tsx
nodes.summary: App-wide settings page managing display (font size, theme), language, recitation and mushaf selection, quiz preferences, azkar behavior, and notification toggles, persisted to localStorage.

nodes.name: loadSettings
nodes.summary: Loads and merges persisted settings from localStorage with defaults, migrating any retired mushaf kind values to the current default.

nodes.name: saveSettings
nodes.summary: Saves settings to localStorage and dispatches a custom 'rafiq-settings-changed' event so same-tab listeners (e.g. PageViewer) can react without requiring page navigation.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: Main Settings page component managing all app preferences with debounced auto-save, a Tajweed rules modal, and sections for display, language, Quran, quiz, azkar, and notifications.

nodes.name: SurahJuzSelection.tsx
nodes.summary: Navigation page for selecting a surah, juz, hizb, or rub el-hizb to jump to in the Quran viewer, with tab-based UI and page-range computation from metadata service.
nodes.languageNotes: Rub el-hizb items are derived from hizb boundary interpolation since the direct getRubStart/getRubEnd helpers were unreliable at time of writing.

nodes.name: SurahJuzSelection
nodes.summary: React component for selecting a Quran navigation target (surah, juz, or hizb/rub), computing metadata-derived items via useMemo and navigating to the viewer on selection.

nodes.name: BottomNavBar.tsx
nodes.summary: Shared bottom tab navigation bar present on all primary screens, with gold-tinted active state, Arabic/Latin dual labels, and navigation to Quran, Quiz, Azkar, Account, and Settings routes.

nodes.name: BottomNavBar
nodes.summary: React component rendering the persistent bottom tab bar, supporting active tab highlighting, optional route override for the Quran tab, and a fixed-positioning mode.

nodes.name: azkarData.ts
nodes.summary: Static data module containing all Islamic azkar (remembrances) organized by category, including Arabic text, source references, and repeat counts, sourced from 'Jami' Sahih Al-Azkar' by Al-Albani.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure data module with no imports; exports a default array of category objects each containing an azkar array with id, text, note, source, and repeat fields.

nodes.name: quran-api.client.ts
nodes.summary: HTTP client for the Quran Foundation Content API, handling OAuth2 token acquisition via a broker, automatic retry on 5xx/429, and data-shape mapping for verses, audio, translations, tafsir, recitations, hizbs, and rub el-hizbs.
nodes.languageNotes: Token caching uses an inflight promise to prevent concurrent refresh storms; 400 invalid_request is treated as 401 on first attempt.

nodes.name: getAccessToken
nodes.summary: Fetches a short-lived OAuth token from the token broker, caches it in module scope, and deduplicates concurrent requests by sharing a single in-flight promise.

nodes.name: apiFetch
nodes.summary: Generic authenticated fetch wrapper that injects the Bearer token, retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff, handles 401/400 by force-refreshing the token, and throws typed QuranApiError on failure.

nodes.name: fetchVersesByPage
nodes.summary: Fetches verses for a Mushaf page from the Quran Foundation API, mapping API shapes to PageVerseDTO.

nodes.name: fetchRecitations
nodes.summary: Fetches available recitations from the API with IDB caching for offline use, falling back to cached values on network failure.

nodes.name: audio-cache.service.ts
nodes.summary: Service that stores per-ayah recitation audio blobs in IndexedDB keyed by reciter:sura:aya, exposing helpers for cached lookup, download-and-cache, and cache statistics.

nodes.name: getCachedOrDownload
nodes.summary: Returns a blob URL for an ayah's audio, downloading and caching it first if not already present.

nodes.name: downloadAndCache
nodes.summary: Downloads an ayah's audio from the API and persists it to IndexedDB; returns true on success or if already cached.

nodes.name: getCachedCountsPerSurah
nodes.summary: Scans only IDB key strings (no blob data) to return a map of surah number to cached verse count for a given reciter, used by the download management UI.

nodes.name: tafsir-cache.service.ts
nodes.summary: Manages the user's downloaded tafsir selection in localStorage and caches the fetched TafsirResource list for offline access.

nodes.name: recitation-history.service.ts
nodes.summary: Persists recitation sessions to localStorage, deduplicating by queue identity and exposing helpers to record, retrieve, and delete sessions.

nodes.name: recordRecitationSession
nodes.summary: Pushes a new recitation session to the front of localStorage history, updating an existing session with the same queue rather than creating a duplicate.

nodes.name: Bookmarks.tsx
nodes.summary: Feature page displaying saved verse bookmarks and recitation sessions, with tab switching, session resume, and navigation to the Mushaf viewer.

nodes.name: Bookmarks
nodes.summary: React component rendering the bookmarks page with two tabs (saved verses and recitation sessions) and handlers for navigation and session resumption.

nodes.name: PlaybackSettings.tsx
nodes.summary: Feature screen for configuring Quran audio playback: verse range selection (by page/juz/surah/hizb), reciter and speed picker, repeat mode toggles, and per-surah offline download management.

nodes.name: PlaybackSettings
nodes.summary: React component providing the full playback settings UI with range pickers, reciter selection, repeat controls, and an offline audio download panel.

nodes.name: expandRange
nodes.summary: Expands a start/end VerseKey pair into an ordered flat array of all verses in between, walking across surah boundaries using chapter verse counts.

nodes.name: TafsirSettings.tsx
nodes.summary: Feature screen for managing downloaded tafsir commentaries, listing available resources grouped by language and allowing the user to save or remove selections.

nodes.name: TafsirSettings
nodes.summary: React component rendering tafsir download management with downloaded and available sections grouped by language.

nodes.name: preload.ts
nodes.summary: Electron preload entry point that bootstraps the Capacitor runtime bridge by requiring electron-rt, making it the first script executed in the renderer's isolated context.

nodes.name: electron-plugins.js
nodes.summary: Empty plugin registry stub that exports a bare object as the slot for registered Electron Capacitor plugins; populated externally by the build or plugin installation process.

nodes.name: electron-rt.ts
nodes.summary: Core Electron Capacitor runtime that iterates registered plugins, maps each class method to an ipcRenderer.invoke call, wires EventEmitter-based plugins with deduplicating add/remove listener helpers, and exposes the complete API surface to the renderer world via contextBridge as CapacitorCustomPlatform.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld to safely cross the context isolation boundary, a best practice for Electron security; EventEmitter subclassing is detected at runtime via instanceof check.

nodes.name: addListener
nodes.summary: Registers an IPC event listener for a plugin class, deduplicating event subscriptions on the main-process side and returning a unique listener ID for later removal.

nodes.name: removeListener
nodes.summary: Removes a specific IPC event listener by ID and notifies the main process to stop forwarding the event type when no more listeners remain.

nodes.name: removeAllListeners
nodes.summary: Removes all registered IPC listeners for a plugin class, optionally filtered by event type, and signals the main process to stop forwarding each affected event.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive project overview covering the tech stack, project structure, navigation routes, audio playback, authentication, CI/CD setup, and IndexedDB schema for this cross-platform Quran app.

nodes.name: SETUP.md
nodes.summary: Quick developer setup reference listing Node version requirements, build commands for development and Android, and APK output paths.

nodes.name: capacitor-electron.config.json
nodes.summary: Capacitor Electron platform configuration specifying the app ID, custom URL scheme, and security settings (nodeIntegration disabled) for the desktop build.
nodes.languageNotes: Sets nodeIntegration to false for security hardening in the Electron renderer process.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Standalone OAuth redirect relay page that extracts the authorization code from the URL and deep-links it back into the Rafeeq app (or local dev server) to complete sign-in.

nodes.name: install.ps1
nodes.summary: Windows PowerShell setup script that verifies Node.js, installs Ionic CLI, React/TypeScript dependencies, Capacitor packages, and conditionally adds Android and Electron platforms.

nodes.name: ionic.config.json
nodes.summary: Ionic CLI project configuration declaring the project name and type as a React app.

nodes.name: privacy.html
nodes.summary: Standalone Privacy Policy page for the Rafeeq app covering data collection, usage, third-party Quran Foundation integration, and contact details.

nodes.name: terms.html
nodes.summary: Standalone Terms and Conditions page for the Rafeeq app covering usage rights, data handling, intellectual property, and disclaimers.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration targeting ES2017 with baseUrl set to src for non-relative imports, JSX react-jsx transform, and strict mode disabled.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses baseUrl: src to allow non-relative imports (e.g. import from 'app/core/...'). Strict mode is off, which is typical for Ionic/CRA projects using TypeScript 4.x.

nodes.name: ic_launcher_background.xml
nodes.summary: Android adaptive icon background layer — a 108dp vector drawable with a solid teal (#26A69A) fill and a semi-transparent white grid overlay used in the app launcher icon.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Android Vector Drawable format (108dp canvas matching the adaptive icon safe-zone specification). The grid lines are decorative and will be clipped by the launcher mask.

nodes.name: ic_notification.xml
nodes.summary: Monochrome notification icon drawable that wraps the launcher foreground mipmap; Android tints it white automatically on API 21+ for the system notification tray.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a <bitmap> wrapper rather than a vector so it can reference a mipmap resource. Android's notification system requires a single-color (alpha-only) drawable.

nodes.name: ic_transparent.xml
nodes.summary: Minimal 1×1dp fully transparent shape drawable used as an invisible placeholder or spacer wherever Android requires a non-null drawable reference.

nodes.name: ic_launcher_background.xml

nodes.summary: Global CSS entry point that imports design tokens, applies a full-height reset on html/body/#root, sets the system font stack and font smoothing, and exposes safe-area-inset CSS variables for edge-to-edge display on Android 15+.

nodes.name: tokens.css
nodes.summary: Single source of truth for the Rafeeq design system â€” defines all CSS custom properties for brand palette, typography (Arabic and Latin font stacks), spacing, radii, transitions, layout, and both night (default) and day theme surface/text/gold/shadow tokens switchable via [data-theme] on the html element.

nodes.name: types.d.ts

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Cloudflare Worker acting as a secure OAuth2 and content-API token broker for the Quran Foundation, holding client credentials as Worker secrets and exposing endpoints for client-credentials token fetching (with in-memory caching) and authorization-code / refresh-token exchange â€” preventing secrets from being exposed to the client app.
nodes.languageNotes: Deployed as a Cloudflare Worker (edge runtime); uses module-level `cache` variable for in-memory token caching between requests on the same isolate instance. Supports both machine-to-machine client-credentials flow and user OAuth2 PKCE authorization-code/refresh flows.

nodes.name: handleOAuthToken
nodes.summary: Handles the /oauth2/token endpoint, exchanging an authorization code or refresh token for user access tokens by proxying the request to the Quran Foundation OAuth2 server with server-side client credentials.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root application component that wraps the entire app in context providers, initializes Quran metadata and page preloading, handles OAuth deep-link callbacks on native platforms, and defines the React Router route table for all feature pages.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Capacitor's App.addListener for native deep-link OAuth callbacks, enabling cross-platform auth flow without a dedicated web redirect.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Root React component that bootstraps context providers, seeds local Quran text corpus, initializes metadata cache, handles native OAuth deep-link redirect, and renders all application routes.

nodes.name: strings.ts
nodes.summary: Centralized i18n translation strings object (STRINGS) containing all UI labels in both Arabic and English for every feature in the app.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large typed object (908 lines) with both 'ar' and 'en' keys at every level, consumed via the useLang hook.

nodes.name: metadata.service.ts
nodes.summary: Core Quran structural metadata service that fetches and caches chapters, juzs, hizbs, and rub el-hizbs from IndexedDB (with API fallback), and exposes page/surah/juz/hizb/rub navigation helpers used throughout the app.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a lazy-init async IIFE pattern — `initMetadata()` must be awaited once at startup; all other exports are synchronous reads from module-level cache variables.

nodes.name: initMetadata
nodes.summary: Asynchronously loads chapters, juzs, hizbs, and rub el-hizbs into module-level caches, reading from IndexedDB first and falling back to remote API calls, then builds the page-to-surah map.

nodes.name: getChapters
nodes.summary: Returns the cached chapters array populated by initMetadata.

nodes.name: estimatePageForVerse
nodes.summary: Estimates the mushaf page number for a given surah and ayah number using chapter page ranges.

nodes.name: getJuzStart
nodes.summary: Returns the starting verse reference for a given juz number by parsing the juz verse-mapping object.

nodes.name: getJuzEnd
nodes.summary: Returns the ending verse reference for a given juz number by parsing the juz verse-mapping object.

nodes.name: getRubNumberForPage
nodes.summary: Computes the rub el-hizb number (1-240) for a given mushaf page number using cached rub data.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyahSetup.tsx
nodes.summary: Setup page for the 'Complete the Verse' (AkmelAlAyah) quiz, allowing users to configure quiz scope (surah, page range, or juz), question count, and time limit before starting.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyahSetup
nodes.summary: Quiz setup component for AkmelAlAyah that manages scope selection (surah/page/juz), page range filtering, question count, and persists settings via Capacitor Preferences before navigating to the quiz screen.

nodes.name: AkmelAlNehayatSetup.tsx

nodes.name: MutashabihatSetup.tsx
nodes.summary: Setup page for the 'Mutashabihat' (similar verses) quiz, extending the shared quiz-setup pattern with multi-surah selection in addition to page-range and juz scope modes.

nodes.name: MutashabihatSetup
nodes.summary: Quiz setup component for Mutashabihat that adds a surah multi-select scope mode on top of the standard page-range and juz modes, saving settings via Capacitor Preferences.

nodes.name: PlaybackContext.tsx
nodes.summary: React context provider that wraps the usePlaybackQueue hook and bridges playback state to Android Auto via the DrivingMode Capacitor plugin, exposing a PlaybackProvider and a usePlayback hook for consumers.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses stable refs to prevent listener re-registration on re-renders, a pattern required because Android Auto listeners are torn down if the effect cleanup runs too often.

nodes.name: getQueuePageMarkers
nodes.summary: Computes one page marker per Mushaf page touched by the current queue, returning empty for single-page surahs or multi-surah queues.

nodes.name: PlaybackProvider
nodes.summary: React component that provides playback controls to its subtree, manages Android Auto synchronization, and handles car action events dispatched from the native plugin.

nodes.name: usePlayback
nodes.summary: Hook that returns the PlaybackControls from PlaybackContext, throwing if used outside a PlaybackProvider.

nodes.name: usePlaybackQueue.ts
nodes.summary: Core React hook that owns a single shared HTML audio element, walks a verse queue with configurable repeat modes, prefetches upcoming verses, integrates with the Media Session API, and records recitation sessions to localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: Careful ref/state split: mutable playback internals use refs to avoid stale closure issues, while UI-visible state uses useState.

nodes.name: probeDuration
nodes.summary: Loads a blob URL into a temporary Audio element solely to read its duration, then tears down the element.

nodes.name: usePlaybackQueue
nodes.summary: Main hook implementing sequenced ayah-by-ayah audio playback with repeat modes, verse prefetching, seek support, Media Session integration, and session recording.

nodes.name: ensureBismillahFont
nodes.summary: Loads and injects the legacy QCF_BSML bismillah font, guarded by a module-level flag.

nodes.name: fetchVersesByJuz
nodes.summary: Fetches all verses in a given Juz from the Quran Foundation API, mapping to PageVerseDTO.

nodes.name: fetchAudioForAyah
nodes.summary: Fetches an audio URL for a single ayah from the Quran Foundation API, falling back to a secondary endpoint on 404.

nodes.name: driving-mode.plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Declares the DrivingModePlugin interface for Android Auto integration and registers it as the RafeeqAuto Capacitor plugin with a web no-op stub.

nodes.name: groupByLine

nodes.name: user-api.client.ts
nodes.summary: Authenticated HTTP client for the Rafeeq user API, providing functions for streaks, activity recording, user profile, notes CRUD, goals CRUD, and local bookmark management via localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses a module-level token cache (tokenState) with expiry check and automatic refresh via a deduplication pattern — only one in-flight refresh at a time.

nodes.name: getAccessToken
nodes.summary: Resolves a valid access token from module-level cache, falling back to token refresh or a token broker endpoint, with request deduplication.

nodes.name: userApiFetch
nodes.summary: Core HTTP wrapper that injects auth headers, handles 401 token refresh with one retry, propagates network failures, and parses JSON responses.

nodes.name: recordActivityDay
nodes.summary: Posts a reading activity session (verse ranges, duration, mushaf) to the API; silently suppresses errors to avoid disrupting user flow.

nodes.name: UserApiError
nodes.summary: Custom error class carrying HTTP status code alongside a message for API error identification.

nodes.name: oauth.service.ts
nodes.summary: OAuth2 PKCE authentication service for Rafeeq — handles code verifier/challenge generation, token storage (localStorage on web, Capacitor Preferences on native), sign-in flow, token exchange, refresh, and sign-out.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange) for secure OAuth2 flow, with platform-aware storage that switches between localStorage and Capacitor Preferences based on runtime platform detection.

nodes.name: storeTokens
nodes.summary: Persists access, refresh, and ID tokens to the appropriate storage backend (localStorage or Capacitor Preferences) based on platform.

nodes.name: signIn
nodes.summary: Initiates the OAuth2 PKCE sign-in by generating a code verifier/challenge, persisting the verifier, and opening the authorization URL in a new window.

nodes.name: exchangeCodeForToken
nodes.summary: Exchanges an OAuth2 authorization code for access/refresh/ID tokens using the stored PKCE verifier, then stores the tokens.

nodes.name: refreshAccessToken
nodes.summary: Uses the stored refresh token to obtain a new access token from the auth server, storing the result and raising SessionExpiredError when the refresh token is absent.

nodes.name: getUserProfileFromIdTokenAsync
nodes.summary: Asynchronously retrieves the stored ID token from platform storage and decodes its JWT payload to extract user profile data.

nodes.name: NetworkError
nodes.summary: Sentinel error class for network failures during authentication, enabling callers to distinguish connectivity issues from auth failures.

nodes.name: SessionExpiredError
nodes.summary: Sentinel error class indicating the user's session has expired and they must re-authenticate.

nodes.name: Account.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-featured account page component displaying user profile, streaks, notes, goals, and settings modals; handles login/logout flow and listens for auth state changes via storage events.

nodes.name: Account
nodes.summary: Main account page component managing authenticated user data (profile, streaks, notes), auth state, and multiple modal sheets for streaks, notes, goals, privacy, about, and feature-request flows.

nodes.name: AccountModal

nodes.name: GoalsCard.tsx

nodes.name: GoalsCard

nodes.name: readSettings
nodes.summary: Reads viewer settings from localStorage and merges them with defaults, providing font size, tajweed mode, translation ID, and other display preferences.

nodes.name: MainActivity
nodes.summary: Capacitor BridgeActivity subclass that bootstraps the Ionic WebView, registers the Rafeeq Android Auto plugin, and configures system window appearance for edge-to-edge display.

nodes.name: onCreate
nodes.summary: Initializes the activity: installs the splash screen, registers the RafeeqAutoPlugin, allows WebView autoplay (required for Android Auto car controls), and applies edge-to-edge styling.

nodes.name: RafeeqAutoPlugin.kt
nodes.summary: Capacitor plugin bridging JavaScript playback context and the Android Auto media service; exposes setContentTree, updatePlaybackState, and jsReady plugin methods, and routes car hardware events back to the JS layer as 'carAction' events with cold-launch buffering.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses @CapacitorPlugin/@PluginMethod annotations for the Capacitor bridge. Companion object holds a singleton instance reference and a pre-launch pending-event slot to survive cold starts.

nodes.name: RafeeqAutoPlugin
nodes.summary: Capacitor Plugin class acting as the JS-to-native bridge for Android Auto; manages a singleton instance, queues events during cold launches, and exposes @PluginMethod annotated handlers for content tree and playback state updates.

nodes.name: storePendingEvent
nodes.summary: Companion static helper that serializes a car action event into a JSObject and stores it as the pre-launch pending event, enabling cold-start event buffering before MainActivity initializes.

nodes.name: load
nodes.summary: Plugin lifecycle hook that registers the singleton instance, picks up any pre-launch pending event from the companion slot, and starts the RafeeqMediaService as a foreground service.

nodes.name: jsReady
nodes.summary: PluginMethod called by JS once the carAction listener is registered; marks the bridge as ready and flushes any queued car event to JS via notifyListeners on the main thread.

nodes.name: setContentTree
nodes.summary: PluginMethod that receives reciter and surah arrays from JS, maps them to typed data objects, and pushes them to the running RafeeqMediaService to populate the Android Auto content browser tree.

nodes.name: updatePlaybackState
nodes.summary: PluginMethod that receives full playback state from JS (playing flag, surah name, verse key, position, page markers) and forwards it to RafeeqMediaService to update the Android Auto media session and notification.

nodes.name: sendCarEvent
nodes.summary: Routes a car hardware action event to JS via notifyListeners when the WebView is alive, or queues it and wakes MainActivity for cold-launch delivery once jsReady fires.

nodes.name: RafeeqMediaService.kt
nodes.summary: Android Auto entry point implementing MediaBrowserServiceCompat; manages the MediaSession, audio focus, a two-level browsable content tree (reciters → surahs), playback state synchronization from JS, media notification with page-navigation actions, and routes car hardware events back to JS via RafeeqAutoPlugin.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses MediaBrowserServiceCompat for Android Auto discovery; detaches MediaBrowser results when content isn't yet available (JS cold launch) and resolves them when JS pushes the content tree via setContentTree. Inner SessionCallback class handles all media button events.

nodes.name: RafeeqMediaService
nodes.summary: MediaBrowserServiceCompat implementation that serves as the Android Auto entry point, managing MediaSession lifecycle, audio focus, a two-level content browser, and foreground notification with custom page-navigation actions.

nodes.name: SessionCallback
nodes.summary: Inner MediaSessionCompat.Callback class that translates all car hardware button events (play, pause, skip, seek, custom page actions, surah selection) into JS carAction events via dispatchCarEvent.

nodes.name: requestAudioFocus
nodes.summary: Requests audio focus from AudioManager using the modern AudioFocusRequest API (Android O+) or the legacy path; dispatches a pause car event on transient or permanent focus loss.

nodes.name: abandonAudioFocus
nodes.summary: Releases audio focus on both Android O+ and legacy API paths, clearing the cached focus request and resetting the focus flag.

nodes.name: onStartCommand
nodes.summary: Handles incoming intents: processes JUMP_TO_PAGE custom actions from notification PendingIntents and delegates all other intents (media buttons) to MediaButtonReceiver.

nodes.name: onLoadChildren
nodes.summary: MediaBrowserServiceCompat callback that serves the two-level content tree: root returns a single 'Choose Reciter' folder, reciters list returns reciter browsable items, and each reciter folder returns playable surah items; detaches results when content hasn't arrived from JS yet.

nodes.name: updateState
nodes.summary: Receives full playback state from JS and synchronizes the MediaSession: sets PlaybackStateCompat (play/pause, position, seek), attaches custom prev-page/next-page/replay-page actions based on page markers, updates MediaMetadata (title, duration), and refreshes the foreground notification.

nodes.name: buildMediaSession
nodes.summary: Initializes the MediaSessionCompat with a PendingIntent pointing to MainActivity, attaches the SessionCallback, activates the session, and sets the session token for Android Auto discovery.

nodes.name: buildNotification
nodes.summary: Builds the foreground media notification with a fixed five-action layout (prev-page, prev-verse, play/pause, next-verse, next-page); prev-page and next-page use PendingIntents to JUMP_TO_PAGE or a no-op intent when unavailable, ensuring stable action indices for the compact media widget.

nodes.name: createNotificationChannel
nodes.summary: Creates the low-importance notification channel required for foreground service notifications on Android O+.

nodes.name: dispatchCarEvent
nodes.summary: Routes a car action to the live RafeeqAutoPlugin instance if available, or stores the event as a pre-launch pending event and wakes MainActivity to handle cold-launch delivery.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Claude Code instructions for the Rafeeq project, directing AI assistants to use the pre-built knowledge graph before resorting to direct file searches.

nodes.name: codemagic.yaml
nodes.summary: Codemagic CI/CD workflow for the Rafeeq iOS release build: installs dependencies, builds web assets, syncs Capacitor, archives with Xcode on an Apple M1 runner, and publishes to TestFlight.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Codemagic's native ios_signing integration instead of manually importing certificates, simplifying the signing step compared to GitHub Actions.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Root npm manifest defining all runtime and dev dependencies, npm scripts for building and running the app on web, iOS, Android, and Electron, plus browserslist targets.

nodes.name: ic_repeat_page.xml
nodes.summary: 24dp vector icon representing the repeat-page (loop) function in the Quran audio player, shown in its inactive state with white repeat-arrow paths.

nodes.name: ic_repeat_page_active.xml
nodes.summary: 24dp vector icon for the active state of the repeat-page toggle in the Quran audio player; identical to ic_repeat_page but adds a filled circle dot at the center to visually signal that page-repeat is enabled.
nodes.languageNotes: Active/inactive icon pair pattern — the active variant appends an extra <path> (2px radius circle) to distinguish state without changing the base artwork.

nodes.name: manifest.webmanifest
nodes.summary: PWA web app manifest declaring icon assets in multiple resolutions (48px–512px) as maskable WebP images for Android home screen and splash screen installation.

nodes.name: getArabicFont
nodes.summary: Returns the optimal Arabic font stack string for the current platform, preferring system fonts on iOS and Android to avoid a network download.

nodes.name: getWorkerCount
nodes.summary: Returns the recommended number of Web Workers to spawn for heavy tasks, capping at 1 on mobile and up to 4 on Electron based on hardware concurrency.

nodes.name: AkmelAlAyahSetup.css
nodes.summary: Styles for the Akmel Al-Ayah (complete the verse) quiz setup page, including surah/juz filter controls, difficulty selector, and start button layout.

nodes.name: AkmelAlNehayatSetup.css
nodes.summary: Styles for the Akmel Al-Nehayat (complete the ending) quiz setup page, mirroring the AkmelAlAyah setup structure with surah/juz filter and start button.

nodes.name: MutashabihatSetup.css
nodes.summary: Styles for the Mutashabihat (similar verses) quiz setup page, including verse pair preview cards, filter controls, and difficulty level selector.

layers.name: App Shell
layers.description: React and Capacitor entry points that bootstrap the Ionic app, configure global routing, and mount all top-level providers

layers.name: State Management
layers.description: React context providers that distribute global state — theme, language, audio playback, and verse visibility — across the component tree

layers.name: Features
layers.description: Screen-level feature modules — Quran viewer, quiz engine variants, bookmarks, azkar, account, tafsir, playback, and search — each representing a distinct app section

layers.name: Shared UI
layers.description: Reusable Ionic/React UI components — Mushaf page renderer, bottom navigation bar, verse action sheet, note modal, and inline select — consumed across multiple feature screens

layers.name: Business Logic
layers.description: Custom React hooks and quiz domain services that encapsulate audio playback orchestration, immersive mode, wake lock, feedback beeps, and the mutashabihat quiz algorithm

layers.name: Data Access
layers.description: API HTTP clients, SDK wrappers, caching services, and IndexedDB persistence layer that source Quran content, reciter audio, tafsir, and user data from remote and local stores

layers.name: Platform
layers.description: Native platform bridges for Android (Capacitor plugin, Android Auto MediaBrowserService) and the Capacitor driving-mode plugin that connect the web layer to device-specific capabilities

tour.title: Application Entry Point
tour.description: The tour starts at `src/index.tsx`, the React entry point that bootstraps the entire application by rendering the root `App` component into the DOM. This single file marks where the browser (or Electron, or Capacitor WebView) first hands control to the React tree. Understanding this file grounds everything else — all routing, all providers, and all features hang from this one mount call.
tour.languageLesson: React apps have a single DOM mount point. `ReactDOM.createRoot` (React 18+) enables concurrent mode features like Suspense boundaries and concurrent rendering, which are relevant if the app defers heavy Quran font loading.

tour.title: Root Component & Providers
tour.description: `App.tsx` is the architectural heart of the app: it wraps the entire component tree in all global context providers (theme, language, playback, verse visibility) and registers the OAuth redirect callback so that sign-in flows complete correctly across web, iOS, and Android. With a fan-out of 5, it imports more modules at the top of the tree than any other file. Reading it gives you a complete map of every cross-cutting concern in the project before you look at any individual feature.
tour.languageLesson: The provider wrapping order in React matters for dependency injection: an inner provider can consume an outer one. In Rafeeq, PlaybackContext sits inside LanguageContext because playback labels may need the current locale.

tour.title: Theme & Language Contexts
tour.description: Two foundational contexts — `ThemeContext` and `LanguageContext` — establish the visual and linguistic environment for every component in the tree. `ThemeContext` toggles light/dark mode, while `LanguageContext` manages Arabic/English switching and drives RTL/LTR layout direction, which is a non-trivial concern for a Quran app where the primary reading direction is right-to-left. These contexts are consumed far and wide, so understanding their shape (what values they expose) helps decode any component that uses `useTheme` or `useLanguage`.
tour.languageLesson: React Context avoids prop-drilling for global values. The pattern `createContext` + `useContext` + a custom hook (e.g., `useLanguage`) is idiomatic TypeScript React: the custom hook enforces that the context is consumed inside its provider and narrows the return type.

tour.title: Verse Visibility Context
tour.description: `VerseVisibilityContext` has the highest fan-in of any node in the graph (4 consumers), making it the most widely shared state in Rafeeq. It controls whether verses are shown or hidden, which is the central mechanic that connects the Quran viewer to all three quiz modes — hiding verses forces the user to recall them from memory. Understanding this context explains how a single piece of state can transform the viewer from a reading tool into a quiz engine without changing the viewer component itself.
tour.languageLesson: Using context to toggle a 'mode' (visible vs. hidden) rather than duplicating rendering logic across features is a clean separation of concerns. The viewer component only needs to ask 'should I show this verse?' — the quiz feature decides the answer by flipping the context value.

tour.title: Offline Storage Foundation
tour.description: `idb.service.ts` is the central IndexedDB singleton that all persistent storage in the app passes through — fonts, audio cache, API response caches, and Quran metadata all share this one database handle. With a fan-in of 2 from `metadata.service` and `usePlaybackQueue`, it is the lowest layer of the data stack. Understanding it once means you understand the storage mechanism for every feature that needs to work offline.
tour.languageLesson: IndexedDB is the browser's built-in key-value/object store that persists across sessions and is available in service workers — essential for offline-first apps. A singleton pattern ensures all callers share the same database connection and migration version, preventing version conflicts.

tour.title: Quran Metadata Service
tour.description: `metadata.service.ts` provides structured Quran data — chapter names, juz boundaries, page mappings — with an offline-first strategy backed by `idb.service`. This service is the foundation for any feature that needs to know 'which surah is this page in?' or 'what are the verses in this juz?' Both the page viewer and the quiz setup screens depend on it, making it a key junction between raw data and the UI that needs it.

tour.title: OAuth & Authentication
tour.description: `oauth.service.ts` implements OAuth 2.0 with PKCE (Proof Key for Code Exchange), the recommended flow for public clients like mobile and SPA apps where a client secret cannot be safely stored. It handles token storage, silent refresh, and session lifecycle — and because it is consumed by both `App.tsx` (for the redirect callback) and `user-api.client.ts` (to attach tokens to requests), it sits at the center of the entire authentication and synchronization system.
tour.languageLesson: PKCE prevents authorization code interception attacks by generating a one-time `code_verifier` and its hashed `code_challenge` before the redirect. The server only exchanges the code if the verifier matches — meaning even if the code is intercepted, it is useless without the verifier.

tour.title: User API Client
tour.description: `user-api.client.ts` wraps `oauth.service` to provide authenticated HTTP calls for all user-specific data: reading goals, bookmarks, and notes. It is the bridge between the backend sync API and the feature screens. By centralizing authenticated requests here, the rest of the codebase never needs to touch tokens directly — it just calls the client and gets back typed data.

tour.title: Audio Playback Engine
tour.description: `usePlaybackQueue.ts` is the core audio engine implemented as a React hook. It manages the HTML `<audio>` element directly, maintains the play queue (individual aya recitations), tracks the selected reciter, and uses `idb.service` to cache audio blobs so recitation works offline. This hook is wrapped by `PlaybackContext` so any component in the tree can trigger playback or query playback state without needing to manage the audio element itself.
tour.languageLesson: Custom hooks that manage a single external resource (here, `HTMLAudioElement`) are the React equivalent of a service class. The hook owns the resource's lifecycle — creating it on mount and cleaning up event listeners on unmount — while returning a clean API to the rest of the app.

tour.title: Playback Context & Android Auto Bridge
tour.description: `PlaybackContext.tsx` lifts `usePlaybackQueue` into the React context tree so that playback controls are accessible from any screen. Critically, it also bridges Android Auto: it listens to `driving-mode.plugin.ts` for events from the car dashboard (play, pause, next aya) and forwards them into the same playback queue. This means a driver can control Quran recitation from the steering wheel without the UI needing any special Android Auto awareness beyond what this context handles.

tour.title: Android Auto Native Layer
tour.description: The Android Auto integration is implemented in two Kotlin files. `RafeeqAutoPlugin.kt` is the Capacitor plugin that bridges JavaScript events to Android's native layer, while `RafeeqMediaService.kt` implements `MediaBrowserServiceCompat` — the Android-required service that registers the app with the car's media system and responds to hardware playback controls. These two files form the complete native side of the pipeline that `driving-mode.plugin.ts` communicates with.
tour.languageLesson: Capacitor plugins consist of two halves: a TypeScript class (the JS-side wrapper) and a platform-native class (Kotlin on Android, Swift on iOS). The `@CapacitorPlugin` annotation registers the plugin so the JS side can call native methods via `Plugins.PluginName.methodName()`. `MediaBrowserServiceCompat` is the Android API that Android Auto uses to discover and control media apps.

tour.title: Mushaf Page Renderer
tour.description: `MushafPage.tsx` is the low-level rendering component for a single page of the Quran. It uses a COLRv1 font — a color-layered OpenType format — to render the Uthmani script exactly as it appears in the physical Mushaf, with each page being a fixed typographic layout rather than a reflow. A `ResizeObserver` adjusts font scaling as the container resizes, ensuring the page always fills its available space correctly across phone, tablet, and desktop.
tour.languageLesson: COLRv1 (Color OpenType) fonts embed multiple color layers per glyph, enabling multi-colored Arabic calligraphy without SVG or raster images. `ResizeObserver` is the modern alternative to `window.resize` events — it fires only when the observed element's size changes, avoiding unnecessary global event overhead.

tour.title: Page Viewer — Core Feature
tour.description: `PageViewer.tsx` is the most interconnected component in the codebase with 16 imports — it is the beating heart of the Quran viewer feature. It brings together `MushafPage` for rendering, `PlaybackContext` for per-aya audio controls, `VerseVisibilityContext` for quiz-mode hiding, and `metadata.service` for chapter/juz lookup. Studying this component shows how all the lower-level pieces assembled in previous steps combine into the primary user-facing experience.

tour.title: Quiz System
tour.description: The quiz system begins at `QuizList.tsx`, which presents the three available quiz modes (Akmel Al-Ayah, Akmel Al-Nehayat, Mutashabihat). Selecting a mode leads to a setup screen — `AkmelAlAyahSetup.tsx` is the entry for the verse-completion quiz, where the user configures which surah or juz to be tested on. Both screens depend on `VerseVisibilityContext` (to control hiding) and `metadata.service` (to list available surahs), tying the quiz system directly back to the foundational layers covered earlier.

tour.title: User Account & Sync
tour.description: `Account.tsx` is the user-facing screen that ties together authentication and cloud synchronization. It uses `oauth.service` to handle login/logout and `user-api.client` to fetch and persist reading goals, bookmarks, and notes. It is the endpoint of the data-access layer — here the user sees and controls all the data that the services underneath have been managing. This is a natural final stop: the tour began with how the app starts, and ends with how user identity and progress are persisted across sessions and devices.
